zh.lifeSummary: Maud De Eyre於大約1244年出生在英國諾福爾可德瓦德。她於大約1265年與Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於1307年10月28日在英國貝德福爾斯特亞特利 煙雨 沙沙享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eyre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: This name was that of a historically prominent family in Galway (Ireland) since the 1650s.

variants.name: Ayer
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English eir, eyr ‘heir’ (Anglo-Norman French heyr, Old French (h)eir, Latin heres). Forms such as Richard le Heyer were frequent in Middle English, denoting a man who was well known to be the heir to the main property in a particular locality, either one who had already inherited or one with great expectations.
English: from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Aier (ancient Germanic Agihari).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Maud De Eyre was born about 1244, in Depwade, Norfolk, England. She married Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe about 1265. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died on 28 October 1307, in Sharpenhoe, Streatley with Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud De Eyre(은)는 대략 1244년 영국 노르폴크 데p웨이드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1265년 Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1307년 10월 28일 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
